text: Writing clean code is an important skill for every programmer. Clean code is code that is easy for other developers to read and understand. One key aspect is using clear naming conventions for variables, functions, and classes. Names should describe the purpose, like *calculateTotalPrice* instead of *calc*. Another important practice is keeping functions short and focused on a single task. This makes the code easier to test and debug. When code is clean and well-organised, it becomes more maintainable. This means you can fix bugs and add new features quickly without introducing errors. In the long run, clean code saves time and effort for everyone working on the project.
